heading: Lightweight materials research and development
label: 17241 Lightweight materials research and development
notes: Effective DateSection effective on the date that is 1 day after , see , set out as a note under , The Congress.Dec. 19, 2007section 1601 of Pub. L. 110–140section 1824 of Title 2

children.text: the development of new materials (including cast metal composite materials formed by autocombustion synthesis) and material processes that yield a higher strength-to-weight ratio or other properties that reduce vehicle weight; and

children.label: A
children.text: lightweight materials (including high-strength steel alloys, aluminum, magnesium, metal composites, and carbon fiber reinforced polymer composites) with the properties required for construction of lighter-weight vehicles; and

children.label: B
children.text: materials processing, automated manufacturing, joining, and recycling lightweight materials for high-volume applications.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: b Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $80,000,000 for the period of fiscal years 2008 through 2012.
